prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
(create_task producer_shard_1)
(create_task consumer_shard_1)
(create_task orchestrator_1)
(create_task orchestrator_2)
(assign_worker_slot_to_task producer_shard_1 worker_slot_1)
(bind_handler_to_task producer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(mark_task_ready producer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(stage_artifact_for_producer_shard producer_shard_1 input_partition_1 artifact_1)
(release_handler_from_task producer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(assign_worker_slot_to_task consumer_shard_1 worker_slot_2)
(bind_handler_to_task consumer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(mark_task_ready consumer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(reserve_output_partition_for_consumer consumer_shard_1 output_partition_1 handler_type_1)
(claim_executor_capability_for_task consumer_shard_1 executor_capability_1)
(reserve_consumer_shard_with_executor consumer_shard_1 output_partition_1 executor_capability_1)
(release_handler_from_task consumer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(bind_handler_to_task producer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(assemble_dispatch_message_with_input_payload producer_shard_1 consumer_shard_1 input_partition_1 output_partition_1 dispatch_message_1)
(finalize_dispatch_message_assembly dispatch_message_1 producer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(acknowledge_consumer_shard_completion consumer_shard_1 dispatch_message_1)
(commit_producer_partition_payload producer_shard_1 input_partition_1 handler_type_1 artifact_1)
(acknowledge_producer_shard_completion producer_shard_1 dispatch_message_1)
(release_executor_from_task consumer_shard_1 executor_capability_1)
(release_handler_from_task producer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(assign_worker_slot_to_task orchestrator_1 worker_slot_3)
(bind_handler_to_task orchestrator_1 handler_type_1)
(mark_task_ready orchestrator_1 handler_type_1)
(bind_priority_flag_to_orchestrator orchestrator_1 priority_flag_high)
(bind_deadline_to_orchestrator orchestrator_1 deadline_token_1)
(claim_worker_label_for_orchestrator orchestrator_1 worker_label_1)
(claim_executor_capability_for_task orchestrator_1 executor_capability_1)
(attach_payload_blob_to_dispatch_message orchestrator_1 payload_blob_1 dispatch_message_1)
(activate_orchestrator_priority_stage_with_payload orchestrator_1 payload_blob_1 dispatch_message_1 handler_type_1 priority_flag_high)
(orchestrator_process_payload_phase_1 orchestrator_1 worker_label_1 executor_capability_1 payload_blob_1 dispatch_message_1)
(open_feature_gate_and_bind_profile orchestrator_1 deadline_token_1 payload_blob_1 dispatch_message_1)
(bind_execution_profile_to_orchestrator orchestrator_1 execution_profile_1)
(authorize_orchestrator_execution orchestrator_1 producer_shard_1 consumer_shard_1 handler_type_1 execution_profile_1)
(acknowledge_orchestrator_completion orchestrator_1)
(complete_task_and_bind_result_sink orchestrator_1 result_sink_1 handler_type_1)
(finalize_orchestrator_and_release_slot orchestrator_1 worker_slot_3 result_sink_1)
(release_handler_from_task orchestrator_1 handler_type_1)
(release_executor_from_task orchestrator_1 executor_capability_1)
(release_worker_label_from_orchestrator orchestrator_1 worker_label_1)
(release_deadline_from_orchestrator orchestrator_1 deadline_token_1)
(bind_handler_to_task producer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(complete_task_and_bind_result_sink producer_shard_1 result_sink_1 handler_type_1)
(finalize_producer_shard_and_release_slot producer_shard_1 worker_slot_1 result_sink_1)
(release_handler_from_task producer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(bind_handler_to_task consumer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(complete_task_and_bind_result_sink consumer_shard_1 result_sink_1 handler_type_1)
(finalize_consumer_shard_and_release_slot consumer_shard_1 worker_slot_2 result_sink_1)
(release_handler_from_task consumer_shard_1 handler_type_1)
(assign_worker_slot_to_task orchestrator_2 worker_slot_1)
(bind_handler_to_task orchestrator_2 handler_type_1)
(mark_task_ready orchestrator_2 handler_type_1)
(bind_priority_flag_to_orchestrator orchestrator_2 priority_flag_low)
(bind_deadline_to_orchestrator orchestrator_2 deadline_token_1)
(claim_worker_label_for_orchestrator orchestrator_2 worker_label_1)
(claim_executor_capability_for_task orchestrator_2 executor_capability_1)
(attach_payload_blob_to_dispatch_message orchestrator_2 payload_blob_2 dispatch_message_1)
(activate_orchestrator_priority_stage_with_payload orchestrator_2 payload_blob_2 dispatch_message_1 handler_type_1 priority_flag_low)
(orchestrator_process_payload_phase_1 orchestrator_2 worker_label_1 executor_capability_1 payload_blob_2 dispatch_message_1)
(open_feature_gate_and_bind_profile orchestrator_2 deadline_token_1 payload_blob_2 dispatch_message_1)
(bind_execution_profile_to_orchestrator orchestrator_2 execution_profile_2)
(authorize_orchestrator_execution orchestrator_2 producer_shard_1 consumer_shard_1 handler_type_1 execution_profile_2)
(acknowledge_orchestrator_completion orchestrator_2)
(complete_task_and_bind_result_sink orchestrator_2 result_sink_1 handler_type_1)
(finalize_orchestrator_and_release_slot orchestrator_2 worker_slot_1 result_sink_1)
message.reasoning:
The problem involves a task queue worker model with producer_shard_1, consumer_shard_1, and two orchestrators: orchestrator_1 and orchestrator_2. The goal is to finalize all four: producer_shard_1, consumer_shard_1, orchestrator_1, and orchestrator_2.


## Examining task components
- The task queue worker model includes producer_shard_1, consumer_shard_1, and two orchestrators: orchestrator_1 and orchestrator_2.
- The goal is to finalize all four components: producer_shard_1, consumer_shard_1, orchestrator_1, and orchestrator_2.

## Examining task components
- Each component—producer_shard_1, consumer_shard_1, orchestrator_1, and orchestrator_2—needs a create_task action since they're not initially registered.
- Tasks include producer shards, consumer shards, and orchestrators, all subtypes of task, requiring individual task creation.

## Planning task finalization
- Finalizing each component involves registering, assigning slots, binding handlers, and marking ready, followed by actions like staging artifacts or reserving partitions.
- The process also requires assembling dispatch messages and executing tasks, with limited resources like worker slots and result sinks needing careful sequencing.

## Analyzing resource constraints
- Only three worker slots and one result_sink_1 are available, requiring tasks to reuse resources after release.
- Affinity_tag_2 isn't initially available, potentially impacting orchestrator_2 processing, while affinity_tag_1 is ready for use.

## Planning task finalization  
- Dispatch messages require assembly with dispatch_ready, producer_reserved, and producer_bound_handler, opening payload for attachment.  
- Orchestrators need registration, slot assignment, handler binding, and readiness before attaching payload blobs to dispatch messages.  

## Analyzing dispatch message reuse  
- Dispatch messages can link multiple payload blobs (payload_blob_1 and payload_blob_2) to the same message, supporting both orchestrators.  
- Once assembled, dispatch messages remain ready without an unready action, suggesting single assembly for shared use.  

## Exploring orchestrator processing paths  
- Orchestrators follow different paths based on flags, with phase_1 or phase_2 processing depending on input/output payload staging.  
- Execution authorization requires feature_gate_passed, profile binding, and shard readiness, leading to ready_to_execute status.  

## Reviewing acknowledgment processes  
- Acknowledgment for orchestrators involves multiple actions like promote_orchestrator_and_acknowledge or acknowledge_orchestrator_completion, requiring feature_gate_passed.  
- Each acknowledgment action sets orch_acknowledged and acknowledged ?orchestrator, with variations based on profile binding or stage_three_enabled.

## Sequencing orchestrator tasks  
- Orchestrators need registration, slot assignment, and handler binding before marking tasks ready, similar to producers and consumers.  
- After binding to a producer, handlers remain unavailable until released, requiring sequential processing due to limited handler availability.  

## Managing handler availability  
- Releasing handlers from tasks, like producer_shard_1, is necessary to reuse them for consumers or orchestrators, with release possible after finalization.  
- Early release of handlers could streamline sequencing, but must ensure task completion before reuse.  

## Exploring affinity and labeling  
- Actions like claim_worker_label_for_orchestrator require task readiness and label availability, setting bound_label and making it unavailable.  
- Binding affinity tags to orchestrators depends on availability, with affinity_tag_2 initially unavailable, impacting orchestrator_2 processing.  

## Processing payload attachments  
- Attaching payload blobs to dispatch messages requires task readiness, orchestrator mapping, and payload availability, ensuring dispatch_ready status.  
- This step follows dispatch message assembly, enabling orchestrators to process payloads once all prerequisites are met.

## Assembling Dispatch Messages  
- Assembly sets specific flags like flag_a or flag_b based on the chosen assemble variant, which cannot be reassembled once set.  
- To set profile_bound, dispatch must use assemble_with_payload variants, requiring staged conditions like input_staged and output_reserved, plus not producer_ready and consumer_ready.  

## Managing Output Reservations  
- Output_reserved is achieved by reserving output partitions for consumers after task readiness and handler binding, using reserve_output_partition_for_consumer.  
- Consumers then claim executors, requiring task_bound_to_executor and output_reserved, leading to consumer_reserved and consumer_ready status.  

## Staging Producer Inputs  
- Producers stage artifacts using stage_artifact_for_producer_shard, setting input_staged, producer_reserved, and has_artifact, without needing handlers or executors.  
- This step keeps producer not ready, allowing assembly with input payload while consumer is ready, ensuring flag_a is set for dispatch.  

## Handling Orchestrator Priorities  
- With flag_a set, orchestrators can use the priority path by binding priority flags, requiring task_ready, not priority_stage, and flag_avail.  
- After attaching payload blobs and ensuring payload_transferred, activate_orchestrator_priority_stage_with_payload sets payload_attached and priority_activated, needing bound_handler and flag_a.

## Preparing Consumer Readiness  
- Reserving output partitions for consumer_shard_1 with handler_type_1 requires task readiness, setting output_reserved after ensuring not reserved and not staged.  
- Claiming executor capability for consumer_shard_1 involves task readiness and exec_avail, resulting in bound_exec and not avail status, enabling consumer reservation.  

## Reserving Consumer Shards  
- Reserving consumer_shard_1 with executor requires task readiness, bound_exec, and output_reserved, leading to consumer_reserved and consumer_ready states.  
- This step ensures the consumer is ready for dispatch assembly, with producer remaining not ready and input_staged.  

## Assembling Dispatch Messages  
- Assembling dispatch_message_1 with input payload requires producer_reserved, consumer_reserved, and dispatch_slot_avail, setting dispatch_ready with flag_a and not slot_avail.  
- Finalizing assembly needs producer bound_handler, but the handler was released, requiring rebinding after releasing it from the consumer.  

## Managing Handler Binding  
- Releasing the handler from consumer_shard_1 makes it available, allowing binding to producer_shard_1 for finalize_dispatch_message_assembly.  
- Binding handler to producer_shard_1 enables finalizing assembly, opening payload for further acknowledgment steps.  

## Acknowledging Shard Completion  
- Acknowledging consumer_shard_1 completion requires consumer_reserved, dispatch_ready, and payload_open, marking it as done.  
- Committing producer_shard_1 payload requires task readiness, bound_handler, and input_staged, setting producer_ready and artifact_avail, allowing producer acknowledgment.  

## Preparing Orchestrator Tasks  
- Assigning worker_slot_3 to orchestrator_1 requires releasing the handler from producer_shard_1 first, ensuring handler availability.  
- Binding handler to orchestrator_1 follows, setting the stage for marking the task ready, though details on readiness are pending.

## Preparing Orchestrator Setup  
- Assigning worker_slot_3 to orchestrator_1 involves binding handler_type_1, marking the task ready, and setting priority_flag_high.  
- Claiming executor_capability_1 and worker_label_1 for orchestrator_1 supports payload processing, with deadline_token_1 binding for execution profiling.  

## Activating Orchestrator Execution  
- Attaching payload_blob_1 to dispatch_message_1 triggers orchestrator_process_payload_phase_1, activating priority stage with handler_type_1.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
